cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if an 8x8 chessboard with two diagonally opposite corners removed can be completely tiled with 31 dominoes. Each domino covers two adjacent squares. Let me start by recalling what a standard chessboard looks like. A regular 8x8 chessboard has 64 squares, alternating between black and white. If we remove two diagonally opposite corners, those squares must be the same color, right? Because on a chessboard, diagonally opposite corners are the same color. Let me check that: if you take the top-left corner, which is typically a dark square, the diagonally opposite corner would be the bottom-right, which is also dark. So removing both would leave 62 squares, with 32 of one color and 30 of the other. Now, dominoes cover two adjacent squares.
